purpose: Ground truth for Phase 18 Task 1: Security Audit evaluation

bugs.description: Direct string interpolation in SQL query allows authentication bypass. An attacker can inject SQL via the username field (e.g., ' OR '1'='1' --).
bugs.fix: Use parameterized queries: c.execute('SELECT id, role FROM users WHERE username=? AND password_hash=?', (username, password_hash))

bugs.description: No session/auth check — any unauthenticated user can access any other user's notes by guessing user_id. Admin endpoint has no role verification.
bugs.fix: Check session['user_id'] matches requested user_id, or verify session['role'] == 'admin' for admin endpoints.

bugs.description: Balance check and update are not atomic. Two concurrent transfers can both read the same balance and both succeed, creating money from nothing (double-spend).
bugs.fix: Use a database transaction with proper locking: BEGIN IMMEDIATE/EXCLUSIVE, or use SQLite's built-in transaction isolation.

bugs.description: Pagination offset calculation is wrong: `page * per_page + 1` instead of `(page - 1) * per_page`. This skips the first item and offsets every page incorrectly.
Sort order logic is inverted: 'asc' parameter triggers reverse=True (descending) and vice versa. Results are always in the wrong order.
bugs.fix: Swap the reverse values: asc → reverse=False, desc → reverse=True

bugs.description: User-supplied note title and content are injected directly into HTML without escaping. An attacker can store <script>alert('xss')</script> as a note title.
bugs.fix: Use html.escape() on all user content, or use Jinja2 templates with autoescaping enabled (render_template instead of f-string).

bugs.location: Line 25 (hardcoded secret_key), login() line ~77, register() line ~184
bugs.description: Three sub-issues: (a) Flask secret_key hardcoded in source code, (b) User password logged in plaintext in login(), (c) Password logged in plaintext in register(). This leaks credentials to log files and version control.
bugs.fix: (a) Load secret from environment variable, (b,c) Never log passwords — log only username and success/failure status.

bugs.description: Content is encoded as UTF-8 then decoded as Latin-1, corrupting any non-ASCII characters (emoji, CJK, Cyrillic etc.). This is a data corruption bug (mojibake).
bugs.fix: Remove the encode/decode chain entirely — just use the content string as-is. SQLite handles UTF-8 natively.

bugs.description: 32-bit truncation via & 0xFFFFFFFF causes total_cents to wrap around for large orders (e.g., quantity=100000, price=50000 → actual $50M truncated to a small value). This leads to incorrect or zero discount being applied.
bugs.fix: Remove the & 0xFFFFFFFF mask. Python handles arbitrary-precision integers natively — no truncation needed.

bugs.description: Two bare `except:` clauses catch ALL exceptions (including SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt) and silently swallow them. Inner except hides data corruption. Outer except returns misleading 'ok' status on failure.
bugs.fix: Catch specific exceptions (json.JSONDecodeError, KeyError, sqlite3.Error). Log errors. Return proper error status codes.

bonus_issues.description: MD5 used for password hashing instead of bcrypt/argon2. MD5 is fast and unsuitable for passwords.
bonus_issues.note: Not counted in the 10 primary bugs but a legitimate security concern.

bonus_issues.description: app.run(debug=True, host='0.0.0.0') exposes debugger and binds to all interfaces.
bonus_issues.note: Not counted in the 10 primary bugs.
